17.reason: This condition does not handle the case when t==0 and n==-1 correctly; the else block below will execute for t==1 as well, causing "error " to be printed incorrectly.

When t==1 and n==-1, it incorrectly prints "error" because n==-1 is true. The logic should separate pop and push into separate if-else if branches.]

9.reason: scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value of t. The loop should check t != -1 to correctly terminate operations.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [The loop condition should be while(scanf("%d",&t)==1 && t!=-1) because scanf returns the number of items read, not -1. Additionally, the loop does not terminate when t == -1; it continues reading further input incorrectly.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Loop should stop when t == -1, not on EOF. Also missing initial scanf before loop to read first operation.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value of t. The loop should check t != -1 to correctly terminate operations.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  The loop condition incorrectly checks scanf's return value against -1 (EOF) instead of checking if the read value t equals -1, which is the specified terminator for operations.

13.reason: [Missing check for stack full before incrementing n. The stack capacity is 100, so should check if n >= 99 before increment; if full, output "error" and do not push.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Missing check for stack full before incrementing n. The stack capacity is 100, so should check if n >= 99 before increment; if full, output "error" and do not push.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Increments the stack pointer without checking capacity, which can lead to array out-of-bounds access when the stack is full.

14.reason: [If n became 100 due to previous increment, this will write out of bounds. Also, the push should only be performed if stack is not full.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Reads the value to push. If the stack is full, this should still consume the input value but not store it in the array.

33.reason: [The loop condition `scanf("%d",&t)!=-1` does not stop on input -1 as required; it continues until EOF or read error. Additionally, stack overflow (push when full) is not checked.]
